title: We Give Thee But Thine Own

w: And glad- ly, as Thou bless- est us, To Thee our first- fruits give.

% 11
W: 6.And we believe Thy Word,
W: Though dim our faith may be;
W: Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord,
W: We do it unto Thee.

metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.
c_lines: Music: 'Energy' William H. Monk (1823-1889). Setting: "Evangelical Lutheran Hymn-Book", 1931.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: We give Thee but Thine own, Whate'er the gift may be; All that we have is Thine alone, A trust, O Lord, from Thee.

2.raw_text: May we Thy bounties thus As stewards true receive, And gladly, as Thou blessest us, To Thee our firstfruits give.

3.raw_text: O hearts are bruised and dead, And homes are bare and cold, And lambs for whom the Shepherd bled Are straying from the fold.

4.raw_text: To comfort and to bless, To find a balm for woe, To tend the lone and fatherless Is angels' work below.

5.raw_text: The captive to release, To God the lost to bring, To teach the way of life and peace? It is a Christlike thing.
